rationale: A0 shows PowerShell launched with '-WindowStyle hidden' executing a Net.WebClient DownloadFile call targeting a bit.ly shortened URL ('http://bit.ly/L3g1tCrad1e') — a classic cradle technique used to obfuscate the true payload destination and download a script to disk. EV-003 confirms the download succeeded, with 'Default_File_Path.ps1' written to the Administrator's home directory by the same PowerShell process (PID 1668). EV-002 shows a PSScriptPolicyTest temp file created by the same process, consistent with PowerShell evaluating execution policy before running the downloaded script. The parent chain (EV-001: explorer.exe → cmd.exe → powershell.exe) with High integrity level and the use of URL shortening to mask the payload source are strong indicators of a staged malware delivery or post-exploitation download cradle.
